summary: Joel briefly recaps a seminar he participated in, noting the large audience of 700 people. He felt his segment on social interface design was too short and could have been expanded significantly. He also observes that the Electric Cloud content, while interesting, was thematically disconnected from his own topic, making the overall seminar feel disjointed.
key_points: The seminar drew a substantial audience of about 700 people
Joel felt constrained by time and wanted to explore social interface design in much greater depth
The pairing of unrelated topics (social interface design and Electric Cloud) made the seminar feel incoherent
money_quotes: I could have talked about this social interface design for hours.
The Electric Cloud stuff was interesting enough but admittedly unrelated to my own topic which made the whole seminar kind of out of whack.
key_insight: Combining unrelated topics in a single seminar undermines the coherence of the event, even when individual segments are strong.
